definition: Matriculant count divided by applicant count for the same legal-residence entity and category. It is descriptive and is not an individual medical school's acceptance rate.
People who selected more than one main race/ethnicity response; kept separate from each alone category in A-10 and A-11.
Men, Women, or All. AAMC states that Another Gender Identity and declined-to-report responses appear only in All.
Applicants applying to U.S. MD-granting medical schools for the first time in the reported academic year.
Alone, In Combination, or Alone or In Combination. Blank for unknown and unduplicated total rows.
Percentage points as published by AAMC, such as 30.8 meaning 30.8%. The denominator depends on the row and response type as defined by the source table.
Acceptees divided by applicants for the same published category. Because race/ethnicity categories can overlap in combination tables, do not sum these category-specific values.
Matriculants divided by acceptees for the same published category.
The entering medical-school academic year reported by AAMC.
